stem: I like learning ideas that are not required for school, simply because they are interesting.
I usually stick to familiar hobbies rather than trying something completely new.
I enjoy imagination-heavy projects—stories, designs, or “what if” debates—more than repeating the same type of task.
I break big assignments into steps and keep track of deadlines without someone reminding me.
My desk, bag, or notes are often messy until the due date is very close.
I double-check important messages or forms before I send them.
Lively group discussions usually give me energy rather than wearing me out.
After a noisy or crowded day, I need a lot of quiet time alone to feel okay again.
I am comfortable starting a short conversation with someone I barely know (for example, before class or in a queue).
Even when I disagree, I try to understand the other person’s point of view before pushing mine.
When I think someone is wrong, I can sound sharp or blunt without meaning to.
I often help classmates who are stuck, even if it slows my own work a little.
Small worries can keep me awake or distracted the night before something important.
When plans change at the last minute, I adapt without feeling thrown off for long.
Useful criticism can still make me doubt myself for a while afterward.
I bounce back quickly after an embarrassing moment in front of others.
